---
output:
  xaringan::moon_reader:
    css: ["default", "extra.css"]
    lib_dir: libs
    seal: false
    nature:
      highlightStyle: github
      highlightLines: true
      countIncrementalSlides: false
      ratio: '16:9'
---

```{r, echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE}
##xaringan::inf_mr()
## For offline work: https://bookdown.org/yihui/rmarkdown/some-tips.html#working-offline
## Images not appearing? Put images folder inside the libs folder as that is the main data directory

library(tidyverse)
##library(readxl)
##library(stargazer)
##library(kableExtra)
##library(modelr)

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE,
                      eval = TRUE,
                      error = FALSE,
                      message = FALSE,
                      warning = FALSE,
                      comment = NA)
```

class: slideblue

.size70[**Today's Agenda**]

<br>

.size55[.center[
Explore Sedgwick's (2004) arguments concerning religious terrorism
]]

<br>

<br>

.center[.size40[
  Justin Leinaweaver (Spring 2022)
]]

???

### Prep for Class
1. ...

<br>

Today's Agenda: Explore Sedgwick's (2004) arguments concerning religious terrorism.

- Is it fundamentally different?

- Do we need special strategies to counter it?




---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-blues_and_greys.jpg')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle, inverse

.size60[.center[**A non-state actor using violence outside societal norms against people or property, meant to intimidate and connected to a political struggle**]]

???

I tried to adapt to your concerns about failed attempts at violence.

IS THIS GETTING BETTER? WHY OR WHY NOT?

MORE TWEAKS NEEDED?



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-blues_and_greys.jpg')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle, center, inverse

.size60[**What explains the variation in how and why non-state actors use violence against states and people?**]

<br>

<br>

.size45[**Q1. How does the framing of this project help us answer our question?**]

???

HOW DOES THE FRAMING OF THIS PAPER HELP US ANSWER OUR BIG QUESTION?

- RESEARCH QUESTION?

- KEY ACTORS?

- KEY INSTITUTIONS?

- KEY PUZZLES?

<br>

Research Question: Is religious terrorism a distinctly different form of terrorism?

<br>

Central to this paper and its framing is the role of Al Qaeda as its central case study.

The question is, how much is AQ a "religious" terror group and how much is it simply a terror group.

Or, similarly to our discussion on Monday, is religious terror distinct enough from other forms of terror as to need to be studied separately?



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-blues_and_greys.jpg')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle, inverse

.size55[.center[**Q1. How does the framing of this project help us answer our question?**]]

<br>

.size60[
Make two lists:

+ AQ is a "religious" terror group
+ AQ is like any other terror group
]

???

Everybody take a few minutes on their own to brainstorm two lists.

*ON BOARD: "AQ = Islamic Terrorism" vs "AQ = Terrorism"*

Think of these like two arguments:

1. IN WHAT WAYS IS AQ A "RELIGIOUS" TYPE OF TERROR GROUP?
2. IN WHAT WAYS IS AQ LIKE ANY OTHER TERROR GROUP?

<br>

* ON BOARD: Gather items under each header * 

("AQ = Islamic Terrorism")

- Religion is central to the way Al Qaeda defines its ultimate aims, its constituency and the way it motivates its operatives.
- Ultimate aims are religious
- Recruits from a specific religious constituency
- Appeals to a vast "potential constituency"
- Uses many religious concepts
- Appropriates ascetic traditions, martyrdom
- 9/11 "Last Night" document to motivate and keep operatives in line
- Constituency defined by Islam
- Adopts the language and stories of Islam to teach its goals
- "When it comes to mobilizing support from within its constituency, mainstream Islamic concepts of duty, ascetic devotion, and piety may be used to political ends" (806).

("AQ = Terrorism")

- HOWEVER, its immediate aims are essentially political in nature.
- Check conclusion re: religious roots of secular terrorism (808-809)
- ?

<br>

ARE WE CONVINCED THAT THIS IS SUFFICIENT TO DEFINE AQ AS A SPECIAL TYPE OF TERRORISM?

- WHY OR WHY NOT?



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/12_2-goal.jpg')
background-size: 100%
background-position: bottom

<br>

.size45[.center[.textred[**Ultimate Aims vs Immediate Objectives**]]]

???

Key to this analysis is that Sedgwick differentiates between ultimate aims and immediate objectives.

WHAT IS THE STRONGEST ARGUMENT TO DO THIS?

<br>

WHAT IS THE STRONGEST ARGUMENT AGAINST IT?

<br>

IS THIS AN ARBITRARY DIVIDE IN ORDER TO SUPPORT THE ARGUMENT OR A SEPARATION OF TWO DISTINCTLY DIFFERENT THINGS?

* Make sure both sides of debate represented *

<br>

WHAT SOURCES OF EVIDENCE DOES SEDGWICK DRAW ON TO SUPPORT HIS ARGUMENT?

- IS IT CONVINCING? WHY OR WHY NOT?

- Non-quantitative piece can make it much harder to assess the evidence provided.
- Very brief historical case studies (797-799) The Thugs, the Assassins and the Zealots
- "propaganda of the deed": Anarchist Errico Malatesta (1853–1932)

<br>

Let's shift to the 9/11 strategy argument.

WHAT IS SEDGWICK'S ARGUMENT AS TO THE STRATEGIC PURPOSE OF THE 9/11 ATTACKS?

(SLIDE)



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-blues_and_greys.jpg')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle, inverse

.size55[.center[**Q2. How does the model of politics presented in this paper help us answer our big question?**]]

<br>

.size60[
9/11 as Strategy

+ Propaganda to build support
+ Provoke self-defeating actions
]

???

(9/11 was primarily a propaganda strategy for building support)

and 

(secondarily an attempt to provoke US into actions that would alienate us further.)

<br>

IS THAT A CONVINCING ARGUMENT? WHY OR WHY NOT?

- ARE THE PROPOSED ALTERNATIVES AN EXHAUSTIVE LIST?

- ARE THE ALTERNATIVES PRESENTED IN THEIR STRONGEST VERSIONS? ANY STRAW MEN?



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-blues_and_greys.jpg')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle, center, inverse

.size60[**What explains the variation in how and why non-state actors use violence against states and people?**]

<br>

<br>

.size45[**Q3. How does the analysis in this project help us answer our big question?**]

???

OVERALL, WHAT ARE THE STRENGTHS AND WEAKNESSES OF THIS RESEARCH ARTICLE?

(Strengths)

1. "propaganda of the deed" is closest thing to a coherent theory of terrorist action, but needs to be fleshed out.

(Weaknesses)

1. Lacks a coherent theory / clearly testable hypotheses.
- How do I use this to look at the world and see if you are right?
- Is "propaganda of the deed" supposed to be a uniquely religious terror strategy or just something AQ does?

2. Historical analogies are interesting but I'm not sure what they prove?
- Ultimately, seems to be that sedgwick argues religious terror is a distinct thing but the three best known examples of it aren't really it...



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-blues_and_greys.jpg')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle, inverse

.size55[.center[**What explains the variation in how and why non-state actors use violence against states and people?**]]

<br>

.size45[
+ Is religious terrorism fundamentally different from other types?

+ Do we need special strategies to counter it?
]

???

ULTIMATELY, ARE WE CONVINCED THAT RELIGIOUS TERRORISM FUNDAMENTALLY DIFFERENT FROM OTHER TYPES?

IF SO, DO WE NEED SPECIAL STRATEGIES TO COUNTER IT?

<br>

THINKING ABOUT THE PAPERS THIS WEEK, ARE WE CONVINCED THAT A TYPOLOGY APPROACH TO TERRORISM IS A USEFUL STRATEGY FOR POLICYMAKERS SEEKING TO REDUCE THE RISKS?

- Nationalist (ETA, IRA), Religious (ISIS, AQ), Ideological leftists (Communists, anarchists), Ideological right (Nazis, skinheads, Klan)

<br>

IF OUR AIM IS TO PREDICT THE BEHAVIOR OF TERRORIST GROUPS, IN WHAT WAYS DOES A TYPOLOGY HELP? IN WHAT WAYS DOES IT HURT?

<br>

IF OUR AIM IS TO DESIGN STRATEGIES TO COMBAT TERRORIST GROUPS, IN WHAT WAYS DOES A TYPOLOGY HELP? IN WHAT WAYS DOES IT HURT?

<br>

DOES A TYPOLOGY ADD SOMETHING USEFUL TO OUR DEFINITION OF TERRORISM OR NOT?
